_meta.batch_description: 7 shared lunar + 8 Saudi (2 fixed + 6 seasonal/lunar) + 7 UAE (3 fixed + 4 seasonal) = 22 entries
_meta.generated_by: claude-opus-4-7 + manual cross-reference against Wikipedia AR, visitsaudi.com/ar/, visitdubai.com/ar/, visitsharjah.com, foundingday.sa, experiencealula.com, diriyahseason.sa
_meta.next_batch: 1C-batch-2: ~28 entries (Qatar + Kuwait + Oman + Bahrain + 5 cross-country shared) — paused pending user approval of this batch
_meta.reference_file_note: The uploaded /mnt/user-data/uploads/gcc_cultural_calendar_comprehensive.json was not accessible to the agent during this batch (Linux-style path on Windows host). Entries were built from the explicit checklist in the Phase 1C prompt + independent web verification. The user may wish to reconcile this file against the upload before approving.

_entry_notes.mawlid_al_nabi: VERIFICATION FLAG: Mawlid is NOT a public holiday in Saudi Arabia (Wahhabi/Salafi tradition rejects celebration of the Prophet's birthday) and is generally low-key/avoided in mainstream Saudi public life. It IS an official public holiday in the UAE and most other GCC states. Modeled here as country_iso=NULL (shared lunar) because the date itself is shared, but content_guidelines.avoid explicitly flags Saudi audience sensitivity. Consider whether to split this into per-country entries in a future iteration, or to drop country_iso=NULL and create AE/QA/KW/OM/BH variants in Batch 2.
_entry_notes.abu_dhabi_art: DATE CORRECTION: Phase 1C prompt listed this as 'Apr, arts/luxury'. The actual event runs in November (2026 edition: 19–22 Nov as 'Frieze Abu Dhabi' — Frieze took over the fair starting 2026; the legacy 'Abu Dhabi Art' brand ran through 2025). Used corrected November dates. Event key kept as 'abu_dhabi_art' for continuity with the checklist. User should decide whether to rename event_key to 'frieze_abu_dhabi' before Batch 2 lands.
_entry_notes.uae_commemoration_day: Marked annually on Nov 30 (date of first Emirati soldier's death, 1971); public-holiday observance was moved to Dec 1 starting 2019. Modeled here with gregorian_month=11, gregorian_day=30 + duration_days=2 to cover both the commemoration itself and the following public-holiday day, since Content Plan surfacing benefits from the wider window. If you'd rather pin to the public holiday date (Dec 1) — say so.
_entry_notes.riyadh_season: Riyadh Season 2025-26 actually opens Oct 10 and runs through ~May 2 (Boulevard World closes May 2). Modeled here as seasonal Oct-Mar per the Phase 1C checklist; this captures the bulk of activity. If you'd like the wider Oct-Apr window for late-season tail, say so.
_entry_notes.alula_season: Officially branded 'AlUla Moments' for the 2025-26 season. The full season runs ~Sep through the following summer per Experience AlUla. Modeled here as seasonal Sep-Apr (8-month window) — wider than the Phase 1C prompt's 'Oct-Apr' to match the official Sep start, but trimmed before summer to keep the active-tourism window honest.
_entry_notes.winter_at_tantora: 2025-26 edition ran Dec 18 2025 → Jan 10 2026. Modeled as seasonal Dec-Jan to match this annual pattern.
_entry_notes.janadriyah_festival: Originally an annual heritage festival run by the Saudi National Guard since 1985; transferred to Ministry of Culture in 2019. 2026 edition referenced in Saudi gov sources but exact 2026 dates not confirmed by an official source page at curation time — using historical Feb pattern. Source confidence set to 'secondary' as a result.
_entry_notes.hajj_season: Categorized under Saudi (country_iso=SA) per the Phase 1C checklist, even though it is religiously observed across all GCC. Rationale: Hajj rituals occur in Saudi territory and are managed by Saudi authorities, so content sensitivity (e.g. avoiding promotional posts during the pilgrimage period) is most acute for Saudi audiences. Eid al-Adha (which immediately follows) remains shared lunar with country_iso=NULL.
_entry_notes.saudi_founding_day: Established as a public holiday in January 2022 by royal decree of King Salman, commemorating Imam Muhammad bin Saud's ascendancy in 1727. Distinct from National Day (which commemorates the 1932 unification under King Abdulaziz). Both are now annual non-religious public holidays.

_translation_notes.ramadan: verified: Arabic Wikipedia uses 'شهر رمضان' or simply 'رمضان' — used 'شهر رمضان' for clarity in content contexts
_translation_notes.laylat_al_qadr: verified: 'ليلة القدر' is the canonical MSA name (Quranic origin, Surat Al-Qadr)
_translation_notes.eid_al_fitr: verified: 'عيد الفطر' — universal MSA, no regional variation
_translation_notes.islamic_new_year: verified: 'رأس السنة الهجرية' — standard MSA. 'العام الهجري الجديد' also seen but the former is more common in calendar contexts.
_translation_notes.mawlid_al_nabi: verified: 'المولد النبوي الشريف' — full formal name. 'المولد النبوي' (without 'الشريف') is also widely used; I picked the fuller form because it's the more reverent/formal version.
_translation_notes.saudi_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني السعودي' — official, matches Saudi gov usage
_translation_notes.saudi_founding_day: verified: 'يوم التأسيس' — official, matches foundingday.sa wordmark
_translation_notes.riyadh_season: verified: 'موسم الرياض' — official, matches Riyadh Season Instagram + visitsaudi.com/ar/
_translation_notes.janadriyah_festival: verified: 'مهرجان الجنادرية' — common shortened form; full official name is 'مهرجان الجنادرية للتراث والثقافة' (Janadriyah Festival for Heritage and Culture). Used the shortened form for UI compactness; full name in content_guidelines.tone if needed.
_translation_notes.diriyah_season: verified: 'موسم الدرعية' — official, matches diriyahseason.sa + visitsaudi.com/ar/
_translation_notes.winter_at_tantora: verified: 'شتاء طنطورة' — official, matches Experience AlUla Arabic site. 'طنطورة' refers to the traditional AlUla sundial, kept as Arabic transliteration.
_translation_notes.alula_season: best_effort: 'موسم العلا' is the direct translation matching the seasonal naming convention (Riyadh Season → موسم الرياض, etc.). The brand also uses 'لحظات العلا' (AlUla Moments) for the 2025-26 program. Picked the seasonal-pattern name for consistency; flagging for user review.
_translation_notes.uae_national_day: verified: 'اليوم الوطني لدولة الإمارات' — official, matches UAE gov usage. Shorter 'اليوم الوطني الإماراتي' also acceptable.
_translation_notes.uae_commemoration_day: verified: 'يوم الشهيد' (Yawm Al-Shahid, lit. 'Martyr's Day') is the official Arabic name. English 'Commemoration Day' is the diplomatic-export rendering; Arabic-language UAE government communications consistently use 'يوم الشهيد'. Using the official Arabic form.
_translation_notes.uae_flag_day: verified: 'يوم العلم' — official, matches mediaoffice.abudhabi/ar usage
_translation_notes.dubai_shopping_festival: verified: 'مهرجان دبي للتسوق' — official, matches visitdubai.com/ar/
_translation_notes.dubai_summer_surprises: verified: 'مفاجآت صيف دبي' — official, matches visitdubai.com/ar/
_translation_notes.sharjah_heritage_days: verified: 'أيام الشارقة التراثية' — official, matches visitsharjah.com Arabic pages
_translation_notes.abu_dhabi_art: best_effort: 'معرض أبوظبي للفنون' is the long-standing Arabic name for Abu Dhabi Art (running through 2025). For 2026 the fair rebrands to 'Frieze Abu Dhabi'; the Arabic 'فريز أبوظبي' is plausible but not yet established in official Arabic communications. Kept the legacy 'معرض أبوظبي للفنون' since the audience association is stronger with that name. Flagging for user review — may need to switch when Frieze's Arabic naming convention solidifies.

avoid: public daytime eating or drinking
loud party scenes or nightclub aesthetics
irreverent humor around fasting
scantily-clad models
alcohol references however incidental
music-heavy posts during daytime hours

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: strongly evening-heavy; daytime engagement collapses; spike around iftar (~sunset) and again post-taraweeh prayer
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: night-centered; supermarket trolleys peak immediately after iftar; fashion/abaya/decor purchases anticipatory in the 2 weeks prior
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: significantly elevated in evenings; Snapchat/TikTok consumption highest in GCC of any month
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: extreme — both at iftar (~sunset) and suhoor (pre-dawn)

year_specific_notes.2026: Ramadan 1447 AH falls in winter (~Feb 18 → Mar 19 2026 per UMQ). Cooler evenings enable outdoor majlis/iftar content; audience more physically active post-iftar than in prior summer cycles.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging
celebratory or party imagery
any framing that overshadows the spiritual significance
humor
prefer: zakat al-fitr reminders for charity sector

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: late-night spike; many Muslims observe i'tikaf (mosque retreat) so social use shifts to brief reflective shares rather than scrolling
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted; spending pivots to charitable giving (zakat al-fitr)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; reflective content outperforms promotional

year_specific_notes.2026: Falls on the night of approximately 16 → 17 March 2026 (27 Ramadan 1447 AH per UMQ). Traditionally observed on one of the odd nights of the last 10 days of Ramadan; the 27th is the most widely-observed canonical date in GCC.

avoid: discount-only messaging without family/spiritual context
generic 'happy holidays' translations
imagery that conflates Eid with Christmas/New Year aesthetics
alcohol references
irreverent humor

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning spike unusual for GCC — driven by Eid prayer and family visit posts; secondary evening peak
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: anticipatory peak in the 7-14 days prior (Eid clothing, gifts); muted during the holiday itself except food/sweets
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: highest engagement day of the post-Ramadan month; selfies, family videos dominate

year_specific_notes.2026: 1 Shawwal 1447 AH ≈ 20 March 2026 per UMQ. Saudi Supreme Court announcement may shift by ±1 day based on moon sighting. Most GCC states observe a 3-day public holiday window.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging
celebratory or party imagery
frivolous humor
any framing that overshadows the day's spiritual weight (it is the pinnacle of Hajj)
prefer: pilgrim imagery from Mount Arafat (where rights-cleared)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: daytime-leaning (many Muslims fast and break fast quietly); spiritual posts outperform commercial
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; quote/Quran verse content outperforms

avoid: graphic depictions of qurban (sacrifice) — sensitive even when culturally relevant
discount-only messaging without family/spiritual context
imagery that crosses into vegan-shaming or celebration-of-meat in a way that excludes other dietary preferences
alcohol references
prefer: themes of charity (one-third of qurban is for those in need)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning prayer + family-visit posts dominate; secondary evening peak; outbound travel posts strong
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: anticipatory peak 7-14 days prior (clothing, gifts, livestock); spending pivots to travel/leisure during the holiday
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high; significant 'away' content (travel, family reunions)
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated for catering and sweets; quieter for individual delivery (people are eating at family gatherings)

year_specific_notes.2026: 10 Dhul Hijjah 1447 AH ≈ 27 May 2026 per UMQ. Falls right after Hajj. 4-day public holiday standard across GCC.

avoid: fireworks/champagne aesthetics (those belong to Gregorian New Year, not Hijri)
loud party imagery
year-end retrospectives that lift Western format wholesale
secular celebratory messaging
prefer: reflective content about the year ahead

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: moderate uplift; less commercial activity than the Eids
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral; no anticipatory spike
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; reflective and historical content outperforms

year_specific_notes.2026: 1 Muharram 1448 AH ≈ 15 June 2026 per UMQ (the Hijri new year that begins during Gregorian 2026).

avoid: FOR SAUDI/QATAR AUDIENCES: celebratory or festival framing — Mawlid is NOT observed publicly in Saudi Arabia (Wahhabi/Salafi tradition rejects it) and is similarly low-key in Qatar. Avoid Mawlid hashtags or congratulatory messaging when targeting those markets.
any imagery of the Prophet (universally avoided across all Muslim audiences)
music-heavy or party imagery
prefer: FOR UAE/KUWAIT/OMAN/BAHRAIN AUDIENCES: themes of the Prophet's life and teachings, mosque imagery, calligraphy of the shahada, community lecture/madrasa scenes
for mixed-GCC audiences: neutral reflective content that doesn't take a position on observance

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: highly country-dependent; strong uplift in UAE/Kuwait/Oman/Bahrain, near-baseline in Saudi Arabia and Qatar
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated in observing countries, baseline elsewhere

year_specific_notes.2026: 12 Rabi al-Awwal 1448 AH ≈ 26 August 2026 per UMQ. SA does NOT have a public holiday; UAE does.

avoid: foreign visual elements (no generic 'patriotic' iconography that could read as any country)
mixing with non-Saudi national symbols (e.g. UAE/Qatar flags in the same composition)
generic patriotic clichés or stock-photo 'celebration' imagery
irreverent humor about the kingdom or royal family
any imagery that could be read as critical of Vision 2030 themes
prefer: Saudi flag (green with shahada) — handled with respect, never on the ground or below the waist
modern Vision 2030 themes (NEOM, Diriyah, Riyadh skyline)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; major fireworks shows in Riyadh/Jeddah drive a sharp peak; #اليوم_الوطني_السعودي trends nationally
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (greenwear), automotive (national-day promotions are a major category), home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in Saudi Arabia for Instagram and TikTok

year_specific_notes.2026: Marks 96th anniversary of the 1932 unification. Government and large brands typically reveal campaigns 14-21 days in advance.

avoid: conflating with National Day (Sep 23) — Founding Day marks 1727 by Imam Muhammad bin Saud, NOT the 1932 unification
modern Vision 2030 skyscraper imagery (use heritage instead — that's the day's spirit)
foreign aesthetics or generic patriotic stock
Saudi flag with shahada (use historical Saudi state flags or Najdi banner motifs instead — Founding Day branding deliberately uses pre-modern symbols)
prefer: ardha sword dance, falconry, equestrian heritage
the official Founding Day visual identity (palm-frond + sword + horse motif)
earthy tones referencing Najd desert palette

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: heritage-themed content outperforms; expect strong Diriyah-related posts; #يوم_التأسيس trends
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional dress, heritage-themed home decor, gold/jewelry
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: high in Saudi Arabia; family heritage content surges
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated for traditional Najdi cuisine (kabsa, jareesh)

year_specific_notes.2026: 5th annual observance since establishment in 2022. Still 'building' as a brand vs. the long-established National Day.

avoid: dated 'Saudi as conservative' framing — Riyadh Season is the headline expression of post-Vision-2030 entertainment opening
any sense that international acts are 'compromising' for Saudi audiences
imagery of the season as foreign-imposed (it is GEA-led, Saudi-branded)
alcohol references (Riyadh Season is alcohol-free)
prefer: Boulevard City and Boulevard World skyline shots
food zone variety (the season hosts hundreds of restaurants)
themed zones (Anime Village, WWE, etc. when topical)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evenings; weekend (Thu-Sat) spike; ride-hailing demand surges around Boulevard zones
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for season-tied merchandise, food/beverage, event tickets
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: sustained 5-month elevation in Saudi Arabia, especially TikTok/Snapchat among 18-34

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition opened Oct 10 2025; Boulevard World extension runs to ~May 2 2026. 11 zones, 200+ concerts, 34 exhibitions, 15 world championships.

avoid: modern/futurist aesthetics (this is a heritage festival, not Vision-2030 imagery)
framing as a 'tourist trap' or staged spectacle
any content that flattens regional diversity into a generic 'Saudi' palette — the festival's whole point is showcasing each region's distinct heritage
prefer: region-by-region heritage themes (Najd, Hijaz, Asir, Eastern Province all represented)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend spike
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional crafts, jewelry, regional food products
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate uplift, strongest among 35+ heritage-interested audience

avoid: lumping it together with Riyadh Season — Diriyah Season is positioned as the heritage/culture counterpart to Riyadh Season's entertainment positioning
modern skyscraper backdrops (use At-Turaif and Najdi mud-brick architecture instead)
loud party aesthetics — this season's vibe is curated, slower, more high-end
prefer: At-Turaif UNESCO heritage site (golden-hour shots are iconic)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; high-engagement content tends to be At-Turaif sunset shots
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for luxury/fine-dining bookings, traditional crafts
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: strong uplift in lifestyle/travel segments; outsized international audience interest

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition runs Nov 1 2025 → Mar 23 2026. Theme: 'Where You Experience Legacy'. Drew 1.3M+ visitors mid-season.

avoid: beach/summer aesthetics
any framing that treats AlUla as a 'desert resort' rather than a heritage destination
modern skyscraper imagery
neon/club aesthetics
prefer: Maraya mirrored concert hall (most-photographed UAE-region structure in 2020s)
Hegra (Madain Saleh) rock-cut tombs at golden hour

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: sunset and concert photography drives strong evening peaks; international/expat audience tilts the timezone profile
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value travel bookings (hotels, flights to AlUla airport); concert ticket purchases
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: outsized international engagement vs. typical KSA event

year_specific_notes.2026: 2025-26 edition ran Dec 18 2025 → Jan 10 2026. Concerts at Maraya, AlManshiyah Carnival, Old Town Nights, candlelit classical concerts at AlUla Fort.

avoid: treating AlUla as 'just a tourist site' — the brand positioning is curated cultural destination, not theme park
summer-month content (the season deliberately ends April when temperatures rise)
imagery that hides the landscape behind crowds — solo / small-group framings dominate the brand voice
prefer: Banyan Tree / Habitas / Our Habitas tented stays

audience_behavior.peak_hours: Sunrise + sunset windows dominate engagement curve
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: landscape-photography-driven; international travel audience visible in engagement timing
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value bookings (premium hotels, charter flights)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate but very high-engagement-per-post (low-volume, high-aspirational segment)

year_specific_notes.2026: 200+ events across the 2025-26 season. Marquee festivals include Winter at Tantora (separate entry), Ancient Kingdoms Festival (Nov), AlUla Tour cycling (Jan 27-31), Desert X (Jan 28-Feb 1).

avoid: any promotional/sales messaging during the pilgrimage window — Saudi authorities are explicit about treating Hajj with reverence
imagery that frames Hajj as 'spectacle' or tourist activity
stock pilgrim imagery without permissioning (Saudi authorities issue official Hajj photography)
showing the Kaaba in any context that doesn't make Hajj/Umrah the subject
any framing that excludes non-pilgrims (most of the local audience are not pilgrims this year)
prefer: respectful coverage of pilgrim journey themes
messages of solidarity with the millions of pilgrims
for non-religious brands: pause cadence on commercial campaigns during the peak days

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: Saudi domestic audience is fragmented (some performing Hajj, others observing); commercial content sees a 5-7 day soft pause
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted on luxury/discretionary; elevated on travel/tour packages for the season
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; spiritual content far outperforms commercial
audience_behavior.food_delivery_spike: elevated in Mecca region, baseline elsewhere

year_specific_notes.2026: 8-13 Dhul Hijjah 1447 AH ≈ 25-30 May 2026 per UMQ. Eid al-Adha falls during this window (10 Dhul Hijjah = May 27). Saudi MoH/MoHU typically issues pre-Hajj guidance in early Shawwal.

avoid: mixing UAE flag with non-UAE national symbols in same composition
generic patriotic stock imagery
any framing that singles out one emirate at the expense of the federation theme — Dec 2 is about the union
humor about the ruling families
alcohol references
prefer: UAE flag (red, green, white, black) treated reverently
Sheikh Zayed founding-father imagery (50+ year anniversary themes)
Burj Khalifa light show imagery (where rights-cleared)
traditional dress (kandura, abaya) alongside modern UAE

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Burj Khalifa light show and Saadiyat/Yas fireworks drive sharp peaks; UAE-wide trends
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for fashion (UAE-color-themed), automotive (major National Day promotions), home decor
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: year's-highest day in UAE; visiting tourist content elevated too

year_specific_notes.2026: 55th National Day (UAE founded Dec 2 1971). Public holiday observed Dec 2-3 standard. Major brand activations typically begin Nov 15 onward.

avoid: promotional/sales-led messaging — this is a day of national mourning, not commercial celebration
imagery that conflates Commemoration Day with National Day (which immediately follows) — they have opposite tonal registers
loud celebratory aesthetics
humor of any kind
prefer: 1-minute-silence imagery (Wahat Al Karama memorial in Abu Dhabi)
respectful UAE flag at half-mast (typical observance protocol)
messages honoring UAE armed forces and emergency services

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning-spike unusual for UAE; driven by the official 11:30 1-minute silence observance
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: muted on promotional content; brands typically pause campaigns
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: elevated; tribute content sees high engagement

year_specific_notes.2026: Commemoration is on Nov 30 (date of first Emirati soldier's death, 1971). Public holiday observed Dec 1 since 2019 — so this entry's 2-day duration covers both. Flag-at-half-mast 8am-11:30am with national moment of silence at 11:30.

avoid: partisan or political framing — Flag Day is consensus-national
humor
any imagery showing the flag below ground level or in inappropriate positions
prefer: themes of unity across the 7 emirates
Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid quotes (he founded the day in 2013)
behind-the-scenes flag-design history (designed in 1971 by Abdullah Mohammed Al Maainah at age 19)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: morning peak (unusual for UAE) driven by ceremony coverage; secondary evening peak
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: neutral (no major commercial moment)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; school/community content dominates

avoid: generic 'sale' messaging that doesn't lean into the DSF brand — DSF is a 30-year-old anchor that audiences expect specific brand cues
any imagery suggesting it's a quiet/curated event — it's a high-energy citywide spectacle
competing with Black Friday framing (DSF positions as the Gulf's equivalent of London/NYC holiday shopping)
prefer: citywide drone light shows and fireworks
raffle/jackpot imagery (cars, gold, cash, vouchers are signature prizes)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; Thursday-Saturday peak; tourist content adds international engagement
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: sharp elevation across all retail categories; international tourist arrivals spike
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: sustained 5-week elevation; concert + raffle reveals drive viral moments

year_specific_notes.2026: 31st edition ran Dec 5 2025 → Jan 11 2026 (~38 days). Future editions follow the same December-start pattern.

avoid: beach/outdoor-activity framing during the hottest hours — temperatures hit 45°C+, audience is indoors
any messaging that treats summer-in-Dubai as a downturn (DSS is engineered to counter the slump)
tourist-only framing — DSS is targeted at residents and intra-GCC travelers, not the December European tourist segment
prefer: air-conditioned indoor experiences (malls, indoor theme parks, hotel staycations)
family-with-kids imagery (school summer break is the target window)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: two-peak daily pattern (mid-morning + late afternoon-evening); audience is families with school-aged children
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated on fashion, kids' goods, electronics; significant staycation booking activity
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; lower-volume than DSF but high-conversion family segment

year_specific_notes.2026: DSS 2026 runs Jul 2 → Aug 30 (~59 days). Organized by Dubai Festivals and Retail Establishment (DFRE).

avoid: modern/futurist aesthetics — this is heritage

any framing that flattens 'Sharjah' into 'Dubai-adjacent' — Sharjah has a distinct cultural identity (UNESCO designated it the cultural capital of the Arab world in 1998 and the Arab capital of culture 2014)
tourist-marketing aesthetic (the festival is for residents + heritage enthusiasts first)
prefer: traditional Emirati crafts (sadu weaving, talli embroidery, palm-frond work)
luqaimat (Emirati sweet dumplings), Arabic coffee ceremonies
international participant pavilions (the festival also showcases other cultures)

audience_behavior.peak_hours: 17:00-22:00 (visitor evenings; pleasant Feb weather enables long outdoor evenings)
audience_behavior.engagement_shift: evening-heavy; weekend spike; family + heritage-interested 30+ segment
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: elevated for traditional crafts, regional food products
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; outsized engagement from Sharjah-resident + UAE heritage segments

year_specific_notes.2026: 23rd edition ran Feb 4-15 2026, theme 'Glow of Authenticity'. Held across 7 locations in the emirate (Sharjah City, Khorfakkan, Kalba, Dibba Al Hisn, Al Dhaid, Wadi Al Helo, Al Hamriyah).

entries.name_en: Abu Dhabi Art / Frieze Abu Dhabi

avoid: mass-market tone — this is a high-end art fair targeting collectors, galleries, and the international art press
treating the event as 'just another art exhibition' — it is the GCC's flagship contemporary art moment
any imagery without rights clearance for the represented artists/galleries
prefer: Saadiyat Cultural District context (Louvre Abu Dhabi, Zayed Museum)

audience_behavior.engagement_shift: high-engagement-per-post but lower volume than mass-market events; international art-collector audience visible in timezone distribution
audience_behavior.shopping_behavior: high-value art purchases (the audience is collectors)
audience_behavior.social_media_activity: moderate; strong Instagram engagement, lower TikTok

year_specific_notes.2026: First edition under the Frieze brand (announced as 'Frieze Abu Dhabi') runs Nov 19-22 2026 at Manarat Al Saadiyat, replacing the legacy Abu Dhabi Art fair that ran through 2025. Organized by Frieze in partnership with the Department of Culture and Tourism — Abu Dhabi.
